records.display_name: 34th Street Transitway (Selected BRT Alternative)

description: Long-term concept for a river-to-river fully separated two-way transitway on one side of 34th Street with options for one-way traffic outbound from midtown, one-way traffic westbound, new plaza and pedestrian spaces, and reserved commercial loading space

Selected BRT alternative for 34th Street following alternatives analysis screening. Two-way Transitway aligned along curb for length of 34th Street. Transitway occupies half of street, north half east of Fifth Avenue, south half east of Sixth Avenue, with a new plaza from Fifth to Sixth. General Traffic: One-Way Eastbound only east of Fifth Avenue, One-Way Westbound only west of Sixth Avenue.
Two-way Transitway for length of 34th Street aligned along curb; single side alignment selected based on street width and corridor needs
34th Street Transitway project presented to Manhattan Community Board 5 as the Locally Preferred Alternative selected by the 34th Street Alternatives Analysis.

Proposed transitway along 34th Street in Manhattan. The meeting covered transit operations, traffic operations, curbside access/deliveries, and three design options for accommodating non-bus traffic flow (eastbound only, westbound only, or outbound only).
Proposed transitway along 34th Street in Manhattan with BRT stations, curb side activity management, and environmental assessment
Transitway project along 34th Street in Manhattan, including Select Bus Service (SBS), bus lanes, off-board fare collection, and pedestrian improvements
Proposed BRT transitway on 34th Street in Manhattan; CAC meeting #1 held June 15, 2010
New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) proposed transitway along 34th Street, collecting detailed information on curbside activities and needs along each block to inform preliminary design
BRT Phase I project. 34th Street Transitway.
